steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the two rivers—not just explain what curviness is or how to calculate it, but state which one is actually curvier.

steps.title: Apply the goal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B — The Embarras River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Luni River. directly compares the two rivers' sinuosity ratios, stating that the Embarras River's is greater than the Luni River's.

The other three either reverse the rivers' sinuosity categories, explain the concept without comparing the rivers themselves, or describe the calculation method without performing the comparison.

That matches Choice B — The Embarras River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Luni River..
